title: WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM - 211info
text: LINCOLN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public health program designed to improve health outcomes and influence lifetime nutrition and health behaviors for women, infants, and children. Nutrition education classes are provided as part of the program; other services include breastfeeding support, monthly benefits for supplemental nutritious foods, and referrals to community resources. 255 SW Coast Highway Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Applicants must meet four criteria to be eligible for WIC: -Live in Oregon (US citizenship is not required) -Be a pregnant, postpartum, or breastfeeding woman, or an infant or child under age 5. Fathers, foster parents, and other guardians can also apply on behalf of a qualifying child. -Have a household income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL). Families receiving OHP, SNAP, or TANF are automatically income-eligible for WIC. -Have a nutritional risk (assessed at time of appointment) Hours: Varies by location Email (541) 265-4112 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 265-4112 Intake (541) 265-4163 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to schedule an appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of ID for parent/guardian and child, proof of address, and proof of income LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Lincoln County Services: WIC Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - HARM REDUCTION PROGRAM HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - HIV/HEPATITIS C TESTING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - BABIES FIRST! INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CACOON INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FAMILY CONNECTS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - NURSE FAMILY PARTNERSHIP If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. LINCOLN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH
